description: Lock state_medical_supply_stores to Roman military infirmary supply provisioning, not generic civic archives or surgical-instrument typology.

checks.reason: The scoped claim is anchored to Roman valetudinaria dated around 100 BCE.
The supply practice is organized inside Roman military medical care settings.
The source names herbs and ointments as stocked medical materials in the field infirmary setting.
Public archival repositories are not the source-backed mechanism for stocked Roman medical materials.
The source can mention instruments without making Pompeian/Roman surgical instrument sets the causal prerequisite.
